title: Friday Sermon 21 March 2003
description:
Verily We sent Our Messengers with manifest signs and sent down with them the Book and the Balance that people may uphold and behave with justice and equity; also We sent down iron which furnishes mighty weapons for violent warfare and benefits for humankind so that Allah may distinguish those who help Him and His Messengers albeit He remains unseen. Surely Allah is All-Powerful, Almighty.

('The Holy Quran.'  Surah al-Hadid [The Iron]: 26).

The denotation  of the root word قوة is multi-faceted, for example it also connotes the inherent power such as that of a kernel that carries in it the power to be a potential tree.

The Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) was the most powerful and courageous among people in that he illustrated this through out his blessed life, in particular in the early days when opposition was the greatest:

Truly, God is Supreme and Powerful. Those who bow to Him with love and steadfastness are certainly not wasted. The enemy means to destroy them by his plans and the evil-wisher intends to crush them. But God says: 'O fool! will you fight Me? Will you be able to humiliate him whom I hold dear?' In fact, nothing can happen on earth except what has been earlier determined in heaven. No hand on earth can extend beyond the measure to which it is allowed to be extended in heaven. Thus utterly foolish are those who plan tyranny, while making their disgraceful and shameful plans, not remembering the Higher Being without Whose Will even a leaf cannot fall.  Consequently, in their resolutions they always remain unsuccessful and humbled. Their evil does not bring any harm to the righteous; rather, the signs of God become manifested and Allah’s creatures increase in wisdom. Although that Mighty and Potent God is not visible to the human eye, yet He reveals Himself by His extraordinary signs.  The attacks of the evil-minded against the righteous have been taking place since ancient times. Prior to myself, the Jews also made the same plan against Jesus, on whom be peace, namely, to falsely declare him a criminal and have him crucified. But look at God’s omnipotence — how He rescued His chosen one!

(Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad - The Promised Messiah & Mahdi.  'Kitabul Bariyyah' ['The Acquittal: An Account of the Exoneration'].  Zia al-Islam, 1898.  'Ruhani Khazain: xiii'.  19, 20.  English Translation, 1996, 1999.  16, 17).

Nay, I swear by the moorings of the stars –

(Al Waqiah [The Happening]: 76).

One aspect of the Divine Powers is exemplified through astronomy. In the universe, the sun is just one illustration of Allah’s power. The benefits of the sun are innumerable, not least that the very existence of life on the earth depends on its energy. Then there are the various features of solar energy the statistics of which elucidate the magnitude of the sun.  The information pertaining to other astral and celestial bodies that are astoundingly bigger and more powerful than the sun further portray the Divine Powers.  The process of photosynthesis employs solar energy by means of which vegetation thrives.

The writings of the Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) explain that all the physical attributes in the heavenly bodies are present in Allah spiritually and eternally. The solar energy, the human faculties, the rain, the vegetation, everything comes to life as a manifestation of His power. These things are not divine but within them there indeed is Divine Power.*

The Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) wrote that the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) was the most perfect manifestation of the Divine attributes, and that just as there are stars around the sun, similarly are his (the Holy Prophet peace and blessings be on him) Companions, who are revolving around him like stars and are getting guidance from his power. The Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) said that Allah the Exalted informed him that the status of his Companions was like that of stars to Allah, in that they all shone, some more than the others, but that they all possessed light and following any one of them would lead one to guidance: 

Narrated Abu Hurayrah: Allah's Messenger ﷺ said 'The strong is not the one who overcomes the people by his strength, but the strong is the one who controls himself while in anger.'

(Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Ismail al-Bukhari.  'Sahih al-Bukhari').

Several incidents from the life of the Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) illustrate his incredible demonstration of patience and trust in Allah in face of disrespect and offence. He said that if he started retaliating in like he would not be able to accomplish his mission. He enjoined never to respond to abuse with abuse.**

* Ahmad.  'Nasim e Dawut'.  (Ziyaul Islam, 1903).  'Ruhani Khazain: xix'.  423).
** Ahmad.  Address to Jalsa Salana 27 December 1901.  'Malfuzat: ii'.  115).
